root_cause.text: Iterating sequentially through all possible intermediate stream IDs to close idle streams consumes excessive CPU time when a client sends a request with a very large stream ID, leading to a potential Denial of Service.
root_cause.evidence: The patch changes the `closeIdleStreams` method in `Http2UpgradeHandler.java` from a loop over all IDs up to the new stream ID (`for (int i = maxActiveRemoteStreamId + 2; i < newMaxActiveRemoteStreamId; i += 2)`) to iterating only over the currently tracked streams (`for (Entry<Integer,Stream> entry : streams.entrySet())`), and a test is added for implicit closes with `Integer.MAX_VALUE - 8`.

vuln_statements.content: for (int i = maxActiveRemoteStreamId + 2; i < newMaxActiveRemoteStreamId; i += 2) {

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: Iterating sequentially through all possible intermediate stream IDs to close idle streams consumes excessive CPU time when a client sends a request with a very large stream ID, leading to a potential Denial of Service.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch changes the `closeIdleStreams` method in `Http2UpgradeHandler.java` from a loop over all IDs up to the new stream ID (`for (int i = maxActiveRemoteStreamId + 2; i < newMaxActiveRemoteStreamId; i += 2)`) to iterating only over the currently tracked streams (`for (Entry<Integer,Stream> entry : streams.entrySet())`), and a test is added for implicit closes with `Integer.MAX_VALUE - 8`.

intent_record.intent_summary: Optimize idle stream closing by iterating only over existing streams to prevent performance degradation with large stream ID gaps.
intent_record.what: Changed the loop in closeIdleStreams to iterate over the streams map entries rather than a numerical sequence of stream IDs.
intent_record.how: Improves execution efficiency by only processing streams that actually exist in the map, avoiding unnecessary lookups for every possible stream ID in the range.

intent_record.intent_summary: Refactor test for implicit stream closure to support parameterized stream IDs, add a test for large IDs, and introduce request timing assertions.
intent_record.what: Refactored testImplicitClose into a parameterized doTestImplicitClose method, added a new test testImplicitCloseLargeId, and introduced timing measurements and assertions for request duration.
intent_record.how: Allows testing implicit stream closure with both small and large stream IDs, and enforces a performance bound on the second request to prevent excessive delays.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update changelog to document the performance fix for closing idle HTTP/2 streams.
intent_record.what: Added a changelog entry referencing bug 64467.
intent_record.how: Updates the project documentation to record a performance improvement for closing idle HTTP/2 streams.

grouping_result.intent: Optimize idle stream closing by iterating only over existing streams to prevent performance degradation with large stream ID gaps.
Refactor test for implicit stream closure to support parameterized stream IDs, add a test for large IDs, and introduce request timing assertions.
Update changelog to document the performance fix for closing idle HTTP/2 streams.

relevance_review.reason: The group's changes directly address the vulnerability by optimizing the iteration over idle streams, changing it from a sequential iteration over all possible IDs to iterating only over existing streams, which mitigates the excessive CPU consumption issue.
The group adds tests specifically for large stream IDs and timing assertions, directly validating the fix for the vulnerability caused by excessive CPU time when closing intermediate streams.
The group only contains changelog documentation updates, which do not address or fix the actual vulnerability root cause in the code.

candidates.content: for (int i = maxActiveRemoteStreamId + 2; i < newMaxActiveRemoteStreamId; i += 2) {
candidates.rationale: This loop iterates sequentially through all intermediate stream IDs, which causes excessive CPU consumption if the gap between maxActiveRemoteStreamId and newMaxActiveRemoteStreamId is very large, leading to DoS.

vuln_statements.stmt_text: for (int i = maxActiveRemoteStreamId + 2; i < newMaxActiveRemoteStreamId; i += 2) {
vuln_statements.explanation: This loop sequentially iterates through all possible intermediate stream IDs. When the gap between the stream IDs is very large, it causes excessive CPU consumption, leading to a Denial of Service.
